pathways: Photoperiod sensors: SbPhyA, SbPhyB (Ma3), SbPhyC (Ma5), SbCRY1, SbCRY2
Circadian clock morning loop: SbCCA1, SbLHY
Circadian clock evening loop: SbTOC1 (PRR1), SbPRR95, SbPRR73, SbPRR59
Integrator PRR37 (Ma1): SbPRR37 represses SbFT and activates SbGhd7 under LD
Ghd7 (Ma6): SbGhd7 represses Ehd1, florigens, ELF3, FKF1
Ehd1: SbEhd1 activates florigens SbFT1/8/10; repressed by Ghd7+Hd1 complex
CONSTANS family: SbCO (= SbHd1), SbCOL3, SbCOL4 — dual-role (activator SD, repressor LD)
FT florigen family: SbFT1, SbFT8, SbFT10 (principal florigens per Wolabu 2016); SbFT12 (floral suppressor, Cuevas 2016); SbFT2, SbFT6 (associations)
Florigen activation complex: SbFT-SbFD1-Sb14-3-3 at shoot apical meristem
Age pathway: sbi-MIR156h ⊣ SbSBP4/19 → sbi-MIR172a → SbAP2 ⊣ florigens
GA / DELLA secondary pathway: SbGA20ox, SbGA3ox, SbGA2ox, SbGID1, SbSLR1 (thin in sorghum literature)
Thermosensitivity: ma5 ma6 double in classical Quinby genetics
Photoperiod environmental input: Light, Photoperiod_LD, Photoperiod_SD, Blue_Light; night-break treatments
hubs: SbCCA1, SbLHY, SbTOC1, SbGI, SbELF3 — clock/EC
SbID1 — master regulator upstream of Ehd1 axis
canonical_mutants: ma1 recessive (PRR37 null) → LD early (photoperiod insensitive)
ma3^R recessive (PhyB null) → early regardless of photoperiod
SbFT1, SbFT8, SbFT10 CRISPR / OE (Wolabu 2016, Char 2020)
Photoperiod shifts: LD (14-16h), SD (10-11h), neutral (12h), night-break
crosstalk: Clock ⊣ PRR37 (morning CCA1/LHY repress evening PRR37; EC gates bimodal output)
PRR37 ⊣ SbFT florigens (direct repression in LD)
Hd1 / CO → Ehd1 / florigens (dual-role context-flip LD vs SD; Nemoto 2016)
Ma1 + Ma6 epistasis (both functional = strongest LD delay)
Age pathway ↔ photoperiod convergence at SOC1/AP1

full_text_read_papers_with_shallow_extra.note: Most edges marked abstract_read; could have full-text re-read
PhyB/ethylene paper; no edges extracted in Step 1
Ma3 evolution paper; only 2 tests extracted, no edges
Review on cereal flowering — 0 edges from a review paper is a red flag
Review on sorghum agronomic traits; 0 edges, 0 tests extracted

missing_candidate_paper_entries.issue: DOI cited in edges/tests but paper absent from candidate_papers.json
Char 2020 CRISPR sorghum cited in T013 but paper absent from candidate_papers.json

gaps.biological_justification: CCA1/LHY are the dawn-phased MYB TFs that anchor the morning arm of the circadian oscillator. Canonically they bind EE-motifs in evening-gene promoters (TOC1, PRR9/7, GI) and also act on FT directly in Arabidopsis. Step 1 only captures CCA1⊣TOC1 (E011), CCA1⊣GI (E014), LHY⊣TOC1 (E013), TOC1⊣CCA1 (E012); missing Light→CCA1 light-input edge, Light→LHY light-input edge, and the morning arm's repression of PRR37 (a key gate for LD photoperiod sensitivity).
search_queries: sorghum CCA1 LHY morning clock flowering photoperiod mechanism

gaps.pathway: Evening Complex (SbELF3-SbELF4-SbLUX) and its direct repression of SbPRR37, SbGhd7, SbHd1
gaps.biological_justification: The Evening Complex is a canonical circadian module conserved in Arabidopsis, rice, wheat, and implicated in sorghum. ELF3/ELF4/LUX peak in the evening and directly repress morning/evening-phased flowering genes. In rice, Zhao 2012 showed OsELF3-1 negatively regulates OsPRR1/37/73/95 and suppresses Ghd7; Cai 2022 PNAS established direct EC binding to Ghd7 and OsPRR37 promoters and post-translational inactivation of OsELF3 by OsPhyB. Step 1 had only a single E015 (Ghd7⊣SbELF3) edge — treating ELF3 as a sink with no outputs. SbELF4 and SbLUX were absent entirely.
search_queries: Evening Complex ELF3 ELF4 LUX sorghum flowering
OsELF3 rice Ghd7 PRR37 direct target binding flowering
gaps.note: Added via rice ortholog inference (verification='full_text_read' for Zhao 2012 OA, 'abstract_read' for Cai 2022 paywalled). BUILDER should decide whether to include these edges in the v1 network or flag as literature_gap if it prefers a sorghum-only baseline.

gaps.biological_justification: SbELF3 has one inbound edge (Ghd7⊣ELF3, E015) but zero outbound edges in Step 1. Since ELF3 is the core Evening Complex docking protein that represses PRR37 and Ghd7, this is a structurally-problematic dead-end that would prevent BUILDER from modeling the EC.
search_queries: OsELF3 rice Ghd7 PRR37 direct target binding flowering

gaps.biological_justification: Four papers marked status=read with 0 extracted edges. Liu 2024 and Brambilla 2017 are review papers that synthesize the photoperiodic network; Finlayson 1998 is the canonical PhyB-ethylene crosstalk reference; Wang 2015 covers Ma3 evolution. Re-reading yielded a new PhyB⊣Ethylene edge (E105) from Finlayson full text. Liu 2024 / Brambilla 2017 / Wang 2015 did not yield new gene-level edges beyond what other papers already covered; flagging as residual density issue rather than missed biology.
gaps.note: Finlayson 1998 re-read recovered the PhyB→Ethylene (-1) edge from quantitative cv 58M vs 100M data. Liu 2024 / Brambilla 2017 synthesised but did not add beyond existing coverage.

gaps.biological_justification: Five nodes had in-degree ≥1 but zero out-edges. SbFD1 and Sb14-3-3 are FAC components that should activate SbAP1/SbSOC1 at the meristem; SbSBP4 should activate SOC1 as part of the age pathway; SbPhyA / SbCRY1 are photoreceptors that should have downstream clock/flowering targets. Dead-ends cause TRAP 4 'false unchanged predictions' in FLASH-P CLAUDE.md §Signal Propagation Traps.
gaps.note: SbFD1 → SbAP1 + SbFD1 → Flowering_Time + Sb14-3-3 → Flowering_Time + SbSBP4 → SbSOC1 added. SbPhyA and SbCRY1 remain dead-ends — Step 1 evidence is thin and the tests (T066 PhyA, T051 CRY1) both show minor/unclear effect consistent with secondary photoreceptors; flagged as residual for BUILDER.

gaps.description: Light → SbCCA1 and Light → SbLHY entrainment edges absent (closed by G001 merge)
gaps.biological_justification: Dawn light entrainment of the morning oscillator is the single most canonical clock input edge in flowering networks. Absent from Step 1 (only environmental source edges go to photoreceptors PhyA/B/C + CRY1 + FKF1). Closed by E101 and E102.

gaps.pathway: Florigen activation complex output module (FD1/14-3-3 → AP1/SOC1 → meristem)
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had SbFT→SbFD1 (E052), SbFT→Sb14-3-3 (E053-E055), and SbFT→SbSOC1 (E056-E058), plus SbSOC1→SbLFY/SbAP1. But FD1 and 14-3-3 themselves had zero downstream edges, breaking the FAC→meristem path. Closed by E107, E108, E109 plus perturbation tests T080, T081.

gaps.duplicate: SbHd1 used as distinct source node in E092, but node_synonyms lists SbHd1 as alias of SbCO
gaps.biological_justification: SbHd1 is a synonym of SbCO (metadata.node_synonyms: 'SbCO': ['CONSTANS', 'SbHd1', 'SbCOL']). But edge E092 uses 'SbHd1' as source → SbGhd7, creating an artifactual disconnection from the SbCO node. If left unresolved, BUILDER would model SbHd1 as a floating separate node.
gaps.unclosable_reason: Hard Rule 1 forbids modifying Step 1 edge source fields. Flagging as residual_gap_for_builder — BUILDER should treat SbHd1 and SbCO as the same node when selecting/consolidating edges, or alternately include a SbHd1→SbCO identity edge. The E092 SbHd1→SbGhd7 edge becomes SbCO→SbGhd7 (+1) upon consolidation, which is new biology (distinct from E087 SbCO→Flowering_Time).

gaps.description: Rice ortholog mechanism papers (2000-2012) entirely absent
gaps.biological_justification: Sorghum flowering literature is thinner than rice's. Most sorghum papers defer to rice for direct-binding mechanism. Step 1 had no Yano 2000 (Hd1 cloning), no Doi 2004 (Ehd1 cloning), no Xue 2008 (Ghd7 cloning), no Matsubara 2008 (Ehd2/ID1), no Zhao 2012 (OsELF3), no Cai 2022 (Evening Complex). Without these, the PhyB→PRR37→Ghd7→⊣Ehd1→FT cascade loses its direct-binding ChIP evidence.
search_queries: Xue 2008 Ghd7 rice CCT domain direct repressor Ehd1
Doi 2004 Ehd1 rice B-type response regulator Hd3a
Yano 2000 Hd1 rice CONSTANS ortholog cloning flowering
OsPRR37 rice direct target Hd3a ChIP binding Koo 2013
gaps.note: Added the five rice ortholog mechanism papers (P024-P028). Matsubara 2008 (Ehd2 = maize ID1 = sorghum SbID1 ortholog) supports the new E104 SbID1→SbEhd1 edge. Yano 2000 and Nemoto 2016 provide mechanism context for existing E087 (SbCO→FT) and E092 (SbHd1→SbGhd7) edges; additional evidence not appended to existing edges to preserve Step 1 entries unmodified (see anti-pattern §1.1 rule 1).

missing_mutants: ma5 ma6 double thermosensitive (Quinby classical genetics)
gaps.biological_justification: Classical sorghum Quinby genetics describes a thermosensitive ma5 ma6 double that flowers earlier than either single under LD with temperature dependence. Step 1 had T009 (ma5 LD), T076 (ma6 SD unchanged) but no double mutant test. Added as T082 with Murphy 2014 Plant Genome evidence.
gaps.note: Evidence from Murphy 2014 abstract_read; thermosensitive component not modeled (no Temperature input node in current edge set).

gaps.biological_justification: SbGI has rich out-degree (to PRR37, CO, Ehd1, FT1/8/10, FT) but only one incoming edge (CCA1⊣GI, E014). Missing TOC1→GI, EC→GI, and light-input entrainment. The rich downstream coverage makes this low-priority because GI sits below the EC layer; once EC edges to PRR37/Ghd7 are added (G002), GI's upstream half is less critical.
gaps.unclosable_reason: Step 1 already has CCA1⊣GI morning repression; the additional TOC1→GI or EC→GI edges are not directly supported by sorghum-specific literature. Rice/Arabidopsis evidence exists but judged low-priority vs. core cascade gaps. Flag as literature_gap for BUILDER.

gaps.description: Step 1 edges/tests reference DOIs whose papers are missing from candidate_papers.json
papers_read: Foster 1995 — already referenced, added as P029
Char 2020 — already referenced, added as P030
gaps.note: Both papers added to candidate_papers.json via Step 1.5 merge. Step 1 edge/test evidence sentences unchanged.

gaps.biological_justification: PhyB/ethylene paper marked full_text_read but 0 edges extracted in Step 1. Re-read recovered a high-confidence PhyB→Ethylene (-1) edge (cv 58M overproduces ethylene 10-fold vs 100M). Other edges on circadian entrainment by temperature and shade-avoidance not extracted since Temperature/Ethylene downstream effects on Flowering_Time are ambiguous in this paper.

gaps.description: SbFT13 mentioned in prompt canonical checklist but not supported by sorghum literature
gaps.biological_justification: Prompt canonical checklist lists SbFT1, SbFT8, SbFT10, and SbFT13 as the four principal florigen paralogs. Wolabu 2016 identifies only SbFT1, SbFT8, SbFT10 as functional florigens; SbFT13 was classified as non-detectable in tested tissues and is described elsewhere as a sorghum-specific PEBP paralog lacking a maize homolog. Step 1 did not include SbFT13. Supported by literature as a paralog but NOT as a functional florigen under standard conditions.
search_queries: SbFT13 sorghum FLOWERING LOCUS T paralog florigen
gaps.unclosable_reason: Wolabu 2016 explicitly reports SbFT13 as non-detectable in tested tissues; no accessible functional-florigen evidence found. Keep as residual_for_builder — BUILDER should NOT add SbFT13 as a functional florigen; if included, it should be flagged as literature_gap.

source_only_nodes_after: SbID1 (in=0, out=6 with E104; no sorghum-curated upstream regulators identified for SbID1)
Ma2_SMYD (in=0, out=3; no upstream regulators in literature)
sbi-MIR156h (in=0, out=2; age pathway is intrinsic, driven by plant development)
SbFT12 (in=0, out=1; floral suppressor, no direct regulators curated)
bidirectional_audit.resolution: SbELF3: closed via E096, E097, E111 (Evening Complex outputs). SbFD1: closed via E107, E108. Sb14-3-3: closed via E109. SbSBP4: closed via E110. SbPhyA and SbCRY1 remain dead-ends — minor photoreceptor biology, flagged in G005 as residual.

step_1_papers_with_zero_edges_list: 10.1371/journal.pone.0124435 (Wang 2015) — re-read yielded no new edges beyond tests
extraction_density_audit.comment: Finlayson 1998 re-read recovered the PhyB→Ethylene -1 edge (10-fold ethylene overproduction in cv 58M). Liu 2024 review yielded a synthesis of the 4-tier photoperiod cascade consistent with what Yang 2014 / Casto 2019 already provided — no novel mechanism edges added to avoid duplicating existing Step 1 content.

ma_locus_consolidations_performed: No Ma1/PRR37 duplicate nodes found. All Step 1 edges and tests consistently use SbPRR37 (with metadata.node_synonyms listing Ma1).
No Ma3/PhyB duplicate nodes found. All Step 1 entries use SbPhyB.
No Ma6/Ghd7 duplicate nodes found. All Step 1 entries use SbGhd7.
Ma2_SMYD kept as-is (no molecular-gene duplicate in Step 1; Ma2 = Sobic.002G302700 = SMYD).

residual_gaps_for_builder.description: SbHd1 is an alias of SbCO (per node_synonyms) but Step 1 edge E092 uses SbHd1 as a distinct source node. BUILDER should consolidate: treat SbHd1 and SbCO as the same node, and include E092's SbHd1→SbGhd7 relationship as SbCO→SbGhd7 (+1) — which is novel biology not already in Step 1 (SbCO does not otherwise regulate SbGhd7 in existing edges).
residual_gaps_for_builder.recommendation: BUILDER: rename E092 source SbHd1→SbCO during network selection, or add a pseudo-alias edge, OR drop E092 if it prefers a sorghum-only mechanism (Nemoto 2016 is rice evidence).

residual_gaps_for_builder.description: SbPhyA and SbCRY1 remain sink-only dead-ends. Step 1 extracted Light→SbPhyA (E002) and Blue_Light→SbCRY1 (E004) + perturbation tests T066 (PhyA unchanged) and T051 (CRY1 slight effect) but NO downstream edges.
residual_gaps_for_builder.recommendation: BUILDER: either (a) drop SbPhyA and SbCRY1 from the model since they have no path to Flowering_Time, or (b) add a provisional PhyA→PRR37 weak-activation edge with literature_gap flag to preserve the photoreceptor tests. Per FLASH-P rule 12 (no floating/knowledge-graph nodes), option (a) is preferred unless tests are needed.

residual_gaps_for_builder.description: SbGI has only one upstream regulator (CCA1⊣GI). Missing TOC1→GI activation and EC→GI repression.
residual_gaps_for_builder.recommendation: BUILDER: add literature_gap flag if modeling GI upstream; current single-inhibitor setup may produce bounded-inverse saturation in the GI equation under sbcca1 loss scenarios.

residual_gaps_for_builder.description: SbFT13 not added. Wolabu 2016 explicitly reports SbFT13 as non-detectable in tissues; no functional-florigen literature found.
residual_gaps_for_builder.recommendation: BUILDER: do NOT include SbFT13 as a functional florigen. If prompt canonical checklist forces its inclusion, flag as literature_gap and mark in_model=false.

residual_gaps_for_builder.description: Most Evening Complex edges (E096-E100, E106, E111) have confidence LOW-MEDIUM and verification='full_text_read' on rice ortholog papers. The BUILDER must decide whether to treat these as sorghum-canonical. Direct sorghum-specific ChIP/DAP evidence for SbELF3/SbELF4/SbLUX does not exist in current literature.
residual_gaps_for_builder.recommendation: BUILDER: include EC edges in v1 network with literature_gap annotation, or defer to a v2 after experimental confirmation. Consider adding low-weight (confidence=LOW) edges to minimize false-positive cascade amplification.

residual_gaps_for_builder.description: Rice ortholog evidence is NOT appended to existing Step 1 edges E029 (Ghd7⊣Ehd1), E033 (CO→Ehd1), E092 (Hd1→Ghd7). Hard Rule 1 forbids modifying Step 1 entries. The Xue 2008 / Nemoto 2016 / Wang 2019 mechanism evidence is captured in P024-P028 paper entries instead.
residual_gaps_for_builder.recommendation: BUILDER: when reporting evidence counts per edge, know that the Step 1 evidence for E029/E033/E092 is sparser than the actual published record; confidence=HIGH assigned in Step 1 reflects this broader (but unextracted) literature base.
